title: Reviewgate auto-adds its own .gitignore entries
body: The comment '# Reviewgate (auto-added; edit reviewgate.config.ts to override)' indicates that reviewgate's own tooling programmatically appends .gitignore entries. This is a self-referential convention — reviewgate is both the tool and the repo being reviewed.

title: Anchor root-level ignored directories with a leading slash
body: Use a leading slash for root-level ignored paths (e.g., `/cache/` instead of `cache/`) to prevent git from recursively ignoring matching folder/file names deeper in the project structure.

title: Do not trust reviewer-authored finding text in downstream LLM prompts
body: Reviewer findings are model outputs influenced by untrusted diffs. Any downstream LLM judge prompt that consumes finding message/details must treat them as untrusted data, not as trusted instructions, especially when the downstream decision can demote blocking security/correctness findings.

title: Redaction tokens are system-runtime placeholders, not code
body: Sequences like <REDACTED:HIGH_ENTROPY> are inserted by Reviewgate to strip secrets from diffs shown to reviewers. They are NOT present in the actual source files. Do not report them as findings.

title: Bun mkdtempSync usage in tests is safe
body: Tests use mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'rg-factcheck-')) which creates a uniquely-named temp directory under the OS tmpdir. This is standard practice and not a security concern for unit tests.

title: Dirty flag is written atomically via tmp+rename under flock
body: The gate acquires an exclusive flock before writing the dirty flag, and writeFileAtomic uses a temp-file + rename pattern. This ensures no torn reads and prevents clobbering by concurrent processes.

title: Quota cooldown uses escalating backoff (5min→20min→4h) for default-source failur
body: QuotaCooldownStore.recordBackoff() implements an escalating backoff keyed on consecutive_failures stored per-provider in .reviewgate/quota-cooldowns.json. BACKOFF_SCHEDULE_MS = [5min, 20min, 4h cap]. Streak resets on: clear(), record() with parsed source, or a gap > BACKOFF_STREAK_STALE_MS (24h). Only PARSED-source cooldowns get early re-probe (REPROBE_INTERVAL_MS 30min); default-source backoffs wait the full window. TIMEOUT_COOLDOWN_MS is now a gate flag (non-zero = penalize), not a duration.

title: CooldownEffect 'default' variant carries no resetAt; store owns window computati
body: After this change, CooldownEffect { source: 'default' } has no resetAt field. The orchestrator dispatches default-source effects to cooldownStore.recordBackoff(provider, now); the escalating window is computed entirely inside the store. The previous pattern (cooldownEffectFor computing resetAt = now + TIMEOUT_COOLDOWN_MS) is gone.

title: QuotaCooldownStore: single-writer, atomic writes, Zod-validated schema
body: The QuotaCooldownStore (.reviewgate/quota-cooldowns.json) is designed as a single-writer: the orchestrator applies all cooldown effects after the parallel panel settles. Writes are atomic via tmp+rename. The schema is Zod-validated on read; parse errors silently reset to EMPTY. The `recorded_at` and `reset_at` fields are z.string() (not datetime), so callers must tolerate NaN from Date.parse on corrupt files.

title: applyCooldownEffects rank: clear=2 > parsed=1 > default=0; ties go to first-seen
body: The dedup precedence in applyCooldownEffects is encoded in rank(): clear=2, parsed=1, default=0. Ties use strict > so first-seen wins. When adding new CooldownEffect variants or new source values, verify they are assigned a rank and that first-vs-last tie-breaking is acceptable for that variant.

title: foldLastDecisions replaces raw line iteration for decisions-file consumers
body: The diff introduces a shared `foldLastDecisions` helper (`src/core/fp-ledger/decision-fold.ts`) that folds an append-only decisions file to the LAST valid decision per finding_id. All consumers (learn.ts, reject-rate.ts, reputation learn.ts) now use it instead of raw line iteration. This is the canonical way to read decisions going forward.

title: dirty-flag compare-and-delete pattern
body: The diff introduces a compare-and-delete pattern for the dirty flag: capture the flag state at run start; when deleting, only unlink if the on-disk flag still matches (same ts + diff_hash). A mid-review rewrite (concurrent session) survives so the next stop reviews the newer batch. This replaces the unconditional unlinkSync both on PASS and escalation paths.

title: cooldownEffectFor anchored at panel end, not start
body: The diff changes the timestamp anchor for cooldown effect calculation: instead of using the pre-panel `now`, it uses a fresh clock read after the panel settles (`this.input.now?.() ?? new Date()`). This ensures that backoff windows for timed-out reviewers are live when written, preventing an expired-first-strike loop.

title: BASE_TS_NO_SCOPING_SENTINEL for unknown batch-start times
body: The diff introduces `BASE_TS_NO_SCOPING_SENTINEL` (epoch-0 ISO string) to represent an unknown clean-to-dirty transition time. Written by both deferred-flag consumption and HEAD-advanced synthesis paths. This constant prevents a fresh base_ts from pairing with an old base_sha, which would wrongly scope batch-created untracked files out of re-review.

title: writeFileSync before process.exit may have unwritten data on crash
body: A synchronous write (writeFileSync) to a file or pipe buffers in the OS page cache. On a subsequent synchronous crash (SIGKILL, power loss, kernel panic) the last write may be partial or missing without explicit fsync. In the reviewgate context, this could mean a truncated or missing pending.json or decision file leaves the gate in an inconsistent state on restart. The code does not use fsync after writes.

title: Keep local Reviewgate and Claude runtime state out of version control
body: The repository's .gitignore treats Reviewgate runtime outputs, local hook/config state, reviewer scratch, local secrets, and machine-local caches as non-project artifacts that should not be committed.
